coreConcept: The water cycle is how water moves around Earth, changing between liquid, vapor, and ice and traveling through the sky, land, and oceans. It is powered by the Sun and helps plants grow, fills rivers, and keeps weather and life working together.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun warms water so it can turn into vapor and start moving through the cycle.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Oceans, Lakes, and Rivers
keyActorsAndComponents.description: These bodies hold most of Earth’s liquid water and supply water that evaporates into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Atmosphere (Air)
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The air carries water vapor and moves it from place to place, helping clouds form and travel.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds collect tiny droplets of water vapor and later release them as rain or snow.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants and Soil
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Plants release water into the air through transpiration and soil soaks up and holds water before it flows or seeps underground.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation and Transpiration
storyOfProcess.description: The Sun heats water in oceans, lakes, and soil and plants release vapor, turning liquid water into water vapor that rises into the air.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: As water vapor cools in the atmosphere, it turns into tiny droplets or ice crystals and forms clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When cloud droplets grow heavy, they fall as rain, snow, sleet, or hail back to the ground.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection and Infiltration
storyOfProcess.description: Fallen water gathers in rivers, lakes, and oceans or soaks into the ground to become groundwater, ready to start the cycle again.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Drives Change
corePrinciples.description: The Sun’s energy causes water to evaporate; without energy, the cycle would not move.

corePrinciples.name: State Changes of Water
corePrinciples.description: Water changes between liquid, vapor, and solid during the cycle, which explains evaporation, condensation, and precipitation.

corePrinciples.name: Conservation of Water
corePrinciples.description: Water moves and changes form but the total amount stays the same on Earth, so it keeps recycling.
